coaching_history.drill: ABANDONMENT CYCLING DRILL: When a parent reveals a child's pain (self-talk, absent parent, loss) — stop and cycle 3× before moving. Use: 'Tell me more about that.' / 'What does that feel like for you as his mum?' / 'How long has he been carrying that?' Do not acknowledge-and-move. Jo Kelly transcript: find the Tyson bomb, practise cycling.

coaching_history.the_moment: Jo Kelly: Tyson abandonment bomb — 'if he was so worried about me, why would he leave me?' This is the moment the close was won or lost. Rye moved past it. If he had said: 'Tell me more about Tyson saying that — how long has he been carrying that feeling?' the call changes. The fact this lead is still warm in HubSpot 5 days later suggests the call built enough connection that she hasn't gone cold. Re-engage this week.
coaching_history.drill: Re-engage Jo Kelly this week. Do NOT pitch. Open with: 'I've been thinking about Tyson since our call. What you shared about him asking why his dad left — I wanted to make sure you're okay, and that he's okay.' Re-anchor the emotional truth. If she's still warm, the second conversation is the close. ONE drill for next DC: when any parent reveals a child's pain about abandonment or a parent leaving — STOP. Cycle twice before moving. The close is IN that moment, not at the end.
